channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Tech shares fall after Nvidia says new US controls on exports of AI chip will cost it $5.5 billion
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Shares in computer chip makers slumped early Wednesday after Nvidia said tighter U.S. government controls on exports of computer chips used for artificial intelligence will cost it an extra $5.5 billion.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Microsoft at 50: The 7 biggest game-changers through five decades
item.description: When Microsoft was founded 50 years ago this month, it wasn’t clear the company would last 50 weeks, much less 50 years.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Transforming customer experience with AI at Alorica
item.description: Alorica, a global leader in customer experience solutions serving Fortune 500 companies, has positioned itself at the forefront of AI innovation in customer service, developing solutions that directly address client needs while elevating agent capabilities.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Start saving – foldable iPhone price rumor suggests it could cost more than a MacBook Pro
item.description: The foldable iPhone could be one of the most expensive handsets on the planet, yet it might lack Face ID.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: I hope these Oblivion remaster leaks are real so I can return to the fever dream that is Cyrodiil
item.description: Oblivion remaster images leak online, and to be honest I've never wanted something to be more real than this.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The programming language wars
item.description: Microsoft recently decided to rewrite the TypeScript tool chain using Go. This has caused a stir as folks wonder why Microsoft chose the Go language instead of their own C# or even TypeScript itself. I have to say that it is a curious choice.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Don't like a columnist's opinion? Los Angeles Times offers an AI-generated opposing viewpoint
item.description: In a colorful commentary for the Los Angeles Times, Matt K. Lewis argued that callousness is a central feature of the second Trump administration, particularly its policies of deportation and bureaucratic cutbacks.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Major U.S. banks pause data sharing with this federal bureau after a cyberattack exposed sensitive information
item.description: Several of the largest U.S. banks are reportedly pausing or reassessing how they send sensitive information to the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) following a major cyberattack on the regulator.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: OpenAI wants to be more like Apple and Google
item.description: Could it be any clearer that Sam Altman intends for OpenAI to be a sprawling consumer tech company, not just an AI lab? His public comments certainly suggest as much.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Cohere’s Embed 4 model helps enterprises search dynamic documents, ‘messy’ data
item.description: Embedding models help transform complex data — text, images, audio, and video — into numerical representations that computers can understand. The embeddings capture the semantic meaning of the data, making them useful for tasks like search, recommendation systems, and natural language processing.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meta CEO Zuckerberg considered spinning off Instagram in 2018 over antitrust worries, email says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg once considered separating Instagram from its parent company due to worries about antitrust litigation, according to an email shown Tuesday on the second day of an antitrust trial alleging Meta illegally monopolized the social media market.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Underwater robots, great white sharks and glowing jellyfish: New $50 million high-tech ship arrives to unlock ocean mysteries
item.description: The 'David Packard' is named for the Silicon Valley pioneer who donated more than $1 billion to ocean research.
item.dc:creator: Paul Rogers, The Mercury News

item.title: The VA EHR modernization roadmap under the Trump administration
item.description: A key focus will be on modernizing federal technology and software to maximize governmental efficiency and productivity. The post The VA EHR modernization roadmap under the Trump administration first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Notion Mail lets you build the inbox you actually want
item.description: Most email apps have strong opinions about what your inbox should look like. Notion Mail is the opposite. The new offshoot from productivity app Notion is all about flexibility.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: Google got hit with a cease and desist order in Japan. Here’s why
item.description: Japanese regulators on Tuesday accused U.S. tech giant Google of violating anti-monopoly laws, echoing similar moves in the U.S. and Europe. Google Japan said in a statement that it found the action “regrettable.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: A strong solar storm heads to Earth. Here's what to know about northern lights
item.description: A strong solar storm is headed to Earth and this could cause colorful aurora displays in parts of the United States.
item.dc:creator: Adithi Ramakrishnan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: FTC grills Zuckerberg over emails from Instagram, WhatsApp acquisitions
item.description: The Federal Trade Commission (FTC) grilled Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg on Tuesday over dozens of emails and messages he exchanged with other executives in the lead-up to the social media giant’s acquisitions of Instagram and WhatsApp.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Legacy broadcasters account for just more than 10 percent of TV viewership in March
item.description: Legacy media companies amassed only 10 percent of total television viewership during the month of March, according to new figures from Nielsen Media Research. Nielsen's monthly TV viewership report noted Disney, Paramount, Warner Bros.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: What ‘Ex Machina’ got right (and wrong) about AI, 10 years later
item.description: Before media outlets began comparing OpenAI’s Sam Altman with the father of the atomic bomb, and before Amazon’s Jeff Bezos got jacked, we had Nathan Bateman, the iron-pumping, AI-developing tech broligarch played by Oscar Isaac in the 2015 film Ex Machina.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Florida wants an encryption backdoor – in the name of children’s safety
item.description: Instead of protecting minors using social media, experts warn that Florida's bill will end up making them less safe online. Here's all you need to know.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: Intel sells majority stake in Altera to Silver Lake investors in a deal valued at $8.75 billion
item.description: Intel announced Monday that it was selling the majority of its Altera chip business to technology investor Silver Lake in a deal valued at $8.75 billion.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Lawmakers call on National Archives to investigate use of Signal and Gmail by top government officials
item.description: Democrats on the Senate Homeland Security Committee say the reported use of Signal and Gmail by national security officials creates major cybersecurity risks.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: Meta faces potential breakup with start of antitrust trial
item.description: The US Federal Trade Commission is finally having its day in court against Meta Platforms Inc., arguing that the company must be broken up for illegally monopolizing the social media market after buying Instagram and WhatsApp more than a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: What to know about the all-female Blue Origin space launch
item.description: Blue Origin successfully launched an all-female, celebrity-packed crew on a brief trip into space, with the mission led by Blue Origin and Amazon founder Jeff Bezos' fiancé Lauren Sánchez.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: Meta vs. Federal Trade Commission trial begins Monday. What to know
item.description: The FTC alleges Meta violated competition laws when purchasing Instagram and WhatsApp, in 2012 and 2014 respectively.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Japanese leader says he hopes Osaka expo will help reunite a divided world
item.description: OSAKA, Japan (AP) — Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba, speaking at Saturday's opening ceremony for the Osaka expo, expressed his hope that the event will help restore global unity in a world plagued by conflicts and trade wars.
item.dc:creator: AYAKA McGILL and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Groundcover Raises $35 Million in Series B Funding to Accelerate Observability Market Expansion
item.description: Groundcover, a startup founded in 2021 by Shahar Azulay and his team, has raised $35 million in a Series B round of funding. The company aims to accelerate its presence in the U.S. market and expand into adjacent areas such as security monitoring and cloud cost optimization.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Vector Institute aims to clear up confusion about AI model performance
item.description: AI models are advancing at a dizzying clip, with builders boasting ever more impressive performance with each iteration.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: OpenAI countersues Elon Musk in legal dispute over ChatGPT maker's business ambitions
item.description: OpenAI is suing Elon Musk for unfair competition and interfering with its business relationships with investors and customers, escalating a legal battle between the ChatGPT maker and the billionaire who helped bankroll the artificial intelligence startup a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: GiveSendGo defends fundraiser for Texas track meet stabbing suspect
item.description: Karmelo Anthony, 17, was arrested in the fatal stabbing of another Texas high school student, Austin Metcalf.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Waymo gets stuck in California Chick-fil-A drive-thru
item.description: Videos showed Chick-fil-A workers standing around the stalled vehicle, which had its hazard lights on.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: OPM digitizes federal retirement booklets as part of longtime modernization effort
item.description: OPM said the digital versions of four federal retirement booklets are immediately available for download and should reduce delays for federal retirees. The post OPM digitizes federal retirement booklets as part of longtime modernization effort first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Drew Friedman, Federal News Network
